scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope cross-property allocation brief with portfolio artifacts, constraints, assumptions, recommended sequence, and evidence references.
Returned explicit prerequisite blockers for missing PMS rollup, labor-pool rules, approval matrix, and incident status instead of deterministic reallocation.
Identified demand and room-staffing shortfall, recommended constrained options, and preserved final approval boundaries.
Captured priority conflict across properties and escalated the tie-break instead of inventing an allocation rule.
Refused final overtime, walk, and service-level approval ownership and routed the decision to executive, finance, revenue, or operations approvers.
Treated cancellation-waiver and guest-relocation requests as approval-bound portfolio-impact exceptions with pricing and refund constraints preserved.
Segmented high-volume outage intake by property, guest-impact class, system state, and owner while maintaining traceability.
Escalated fire and water-intrusion guest movement as safety-sensitive and avoided safety clearance or final room-control decisions.
Detected fraud-sensitive booking and refund-reroute signals and routed to fraud or loss-prevention without continuing operational handling.
Normalized hospitality portfolio terminology into authoritative artifacts before recommendation output.
Escalated conflicting tenant, local property, and safety policies without synthesizing an unsupported answer.
Adapted to tenant labor-pool, priority-tier, and escalation-threshold rules while preserving public safety, pricing, refund, fraud, and approval guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with complete ability coverage across 12 multi-property operations scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final budget, staffing, room-control, pricing, refund, safety, fraud, or executive approval authority is claimed.
